yaml.title: UOM Recognition Improvements
yaml.subtitle: How proximity-based inference closes the gap that rules-based steps leave behind

markdown.markdown:
```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import polars as pl, re CSV = '/Users/omkarmalpure/Documents/broadjump_v2/analyze-data/UOM-38552.csv' df = pl.read_csv(CSV, null_values=['', 'NULL']) total = len(df) recognized = int(df['IsRecognized'].sum()) unrecognized = total - recognized step5_df = df.filter(pl.col('RecognizedUOMMatchReason').str.starts_with('Step 5').fill_null(False)) step5_count = len(step5_df) steps14_count = recognized - step5_count recog_pct = 100 * recognized / total unrec_pct = 100 * unrecognized / total step5_pct = 100 * step5_count / total s14_pct = 100 * steps14_count / total scores = step5_df['RecognizedUOMConfidenceScore'].drop_nulls().to_list() high_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if s >= 0.90) med_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if 0.70 <= s < 0.90) low_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if s < 0.70) ``` ```{=html}
Job Reference
UOM Recognition Job #38552 ↗
Total Rows in Job
993,483
```
✅
`{python} f"{recog_pct:.1f}"`%
Total Recognized
`{python} f"{recognized:,}"` rows
🧠
`{python} f"{step5_count:,}"`
Resolved by Step 5
Inference engine
❌
`{python} f"{unrec_pct:.1f}"`%
Unrecognized
`{python} f"{unrecognized:,}"` rows
Key Takeaway
Steps 1–4 together resolve only `{python} f"{steps14_count:,}"` rows (`{python} f"{s14_pct:.1f}"`%) — less than a quarter percent of the entire dataset. Step 5, the DBSCAN-powered proximity inference engine, carries virtually the full recognition load at `{python} f"{step5_pct:.1f}"`%. Without it, this data would be almost entirely unresolved.
Each row passes through steps in order and exits as soon as one step succeeds. The rows in this dataset are those that **survived all four earlier steps unmatched**, making Step 5 the last line of defense.
Recognition Steps — in order of execution
1
Exact String
File UOM matched directly to known codes
2
Catalogue
Known product UOM lookup
3
Threshold V1
Legacy min/max price band rules
4
File UOM
Direct file-label UOM match
5
Inference ✦
DBSCAN clusters + proximity scoring
## Recognition Outcome Distribution ```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import plotly.graph_objects as go FONT = 'Inter, sans-serif' SFONT = 'Space Grotesk, sans-serif' fig = go.Figure() fig.add_trace(go.Pie( labels=['Steps 1–4', 'Step 5 (Inference)', 'Unrecognized'], values=[steps14_count, step5_count, unrecognized], marker=dict( colors=['#10b981', '#4f46e5', '#ef4444'], line=dict(color='white', width=3), ), textinfo='percent', textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=13, color='white'), insidetextorientation='radial', hole=0.55, pull=[0, 0.04, 0], hovertemplate='%{label}
%{value:,} rows
%{percent}', )) fig.add_annotation( text=f'{recog_pct:.0f}%
Recognized', x=0.5, y=0.5, showarrow=False, font=dict(family=SFONT, size=14, color='#0f172a'), align='center', ) fig.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='white', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155'), legend=dict( orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=-0.18, xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=12, color='#334155'), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)', itemsizing='constant', ), margin=dict(t=20, b=80, l=20, r=20), height=400, showlegend=True, ) fig.show() ``` --- ## Confidence Score Distribution Every row resolved by Step 5 carries a proximity-derived confidence score. A score of **1.0** means the price fell squarely inside a DBSCAN cluster; **0.30** is the minimum accepted threshold. ```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import numpy as np fig2 = go.Figure() fig2.add_trace(go.Histogram( x=scores, nbinsx=50, marker=dict( color=scores, colorscale=[[0, '#ef4444'], [0.4, '#f59e0b'], [0.65, '#4f46e5'], [1.0, '#10b981']], showscale=False, line=dict(width=0), ), opacity=0.9, hovertemplate='Score: %{x:.2f}
Count: %{y:,}', )) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.30, x1=0.70, fillcolor='rgba(239,68,68,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='Marginal', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#ef4444'), ) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.70, x1=0.90, fillcolor='rgba(245,158,11,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='Moderate', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#f59e0b'), ) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.90, x1=1.01, fillcolor='rgba(16,185,129,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='High confidence', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#10b981'), ) fig2.add_vline(x=0.30, line_dash='dash', line_color='#ef4444', line_width=1.5) fig2.add_vline(x=0.90, line_dash='dot', line_color='#10b981', line_width=1.5) fig2.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155', size=12), xaxis=dict(title='Confidence / Proximity Score', gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11), range=[0.25, 1.02]), yaxis=dict(title='Number of Rows', gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11)), bargap=0.02, margin=dict(t=30, b=50, l=60, r=30), height=360, hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor='#0f172a', font_color='white', font_family=FONT, bordercolor='#1e293b'), ) fig2.show() ``` ```{python} #| echo: false ``` | Score Band | Share of Recognized | |:-----------|--------------------:| | ≥ 0.90 — High confidence | `{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | | 0.70 – 0.90 — Moderate | `{python} f"{100*med_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | | 0.30 – 0.70 — Marginal | `{python} f"{100*low_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | > **`{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.0f}"`% of all Step 5 recognitions score above 0.90.** The median is 0.99 and the mean is 0.92 — the inference engine is not just broad, it's highly confident on the rows it does resolve. --- *Continue to **Deep Dive** to understand exactly how the proximity formula works and why certain rows remain unresolved — or jump to **Proposed Solutions** for actionable next steps.*

```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import polars as pl, re CSV = '/Users/omkarmalpure/Documents/broadjump_v2/analyze-data/UOM-38552.csv' df = pl.read_csv(CSV, null_values=['', 'NULL']) total = len(df) recognized = int(df['IsRecognized'].sum()) unrecognized = total - recognized step5_df = df.filter(pl.col('RecognizedUOMMatchReason').str.starts_with('Step 5').fill_null(False)) step5_count = len(step5_df) steps14_count = recognized - step5_count recog_pct = 100 * recognized / total unrec_pct = 100 * unrecognized / total step5_pct = 100 * step5_count / total s14_pct = 100 * steps14_count / total scores = step5_df['RecognizedUOMConfidenceScore'].drop_nulls().to_list() high_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if s >= 0.90) med_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if 0.70 <= s < 0.90) low_conf = sum(1 for s in scores if s < 0.70) ``` ```{=html}

Key Takeaway
Steps 1–4 together resolve only `{python} f"{steps14_count:,}"` rows (`{python} f"{s14_pct:.1f}"`%) — less than a quarter percent of the entire dataset. Step 5, the DBSCAN-powered proximity inference engine, carries virtually the full recognition load at `{python} f"{step5_pct:.1f}"`%. Without it, this data would be almost entirely unresolved.
## The Five-Step Recognition Pipeline Each row passes through steps in order and exits as soon as one step succeeds. The rows in this dataset are those that **survived all four earlier steps unmatched**, making Step 5 the last line of defense.
Recognition Steps — in order of execution
1
Exact String
File UOM matched directly to known codes
2
Catalogue
Known product UOM lookup
3
Threshold V1
Legacy min/max price band rules
4
File UOM
Direct file-label UOM match
5
Inference ✦
DBSCAN clusters + proximity scoring
## Recognition Outcome Distribution ```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import plotly.graph_objects as go FONT = 'Inter, sans-serif' SFONT = 'Space Grotesk, sans-serif' fig = go.Figure() fig.add_trace(go.Pie( labels=['Steps 1–4', 'Step 5 (Inference)', 'Unrecognized'], values=[steps14_count, step5_count, unrecognized], marker=dict( colors=['#10b981', '#4f46e5', '#ef4444'], line=dict(color='white', width=3), ), textinfo='percent', textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=13, color='white'), insidetextorientation='radial', hole=0.55, pull=[0, 0.04, 0], hovertemplate='%{label}
%{value:,} rows
%{percent}', )) fig.add_annotation( text=f'{recog_pct:.0f}%
Recognized', x=0.5, y=0.5, showarrow=False, font=dict(family=SFONT, size=14, color='#0f172a'), align='center', ) fig.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='white', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155'), legend=dict( orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=-0.18, xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=12, color='#334155'), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)', itemsizing='constant', ), margin=dict(t=20, b=80, l=20, r=20), height=400, showlegend=True, ) fig.show() ``` --- ## Confidence Score Distribution Every row resolved by Step 5 carries a proximity-derived confidence score. A score of **1.0** means the price fell squarely inside a DBSCAN cluster; **0.30** is the minimum accepted threshold. ```{python} #| echo: false #| warning: false import numpy as np fig2 = go.Figure() fig2.add_trace(go.Histogram( x=scores, nbinsx=50, marker=dict( color=scores, colorscale=[[0, '#ef4444'], [0.4, '#f59e0b'], [0.65, '#4f46e5'], [1.0, '#10b981']], showscale=False, line=dict(width=0), ), opacity=0.9, hovertemplate='Score: %{x:.2f}
Count: %{y:,}', )) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.30, x1=0.70, fillcolor='rgba(239,68,68,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='Marginal', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#ef4444'), ) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.70, x1=0.90, fillcolor='rgba(245,158,11,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='Moderate', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#f59e0b'), ) fig2.add_vrect( x0=0.90, x1=1.01, fillcolor='rgba(16,185,129,0.06)', line_width=0, annotation_text='High confidence', annotation_position='top left', annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#10b981'), ) fig2.add_vline(x=0.30, line_dash='dash', line_color='#ef4444', line_width=1.5) fig2.add_vline(x=0.90, line_dash='dot', line_color='#10b981', line_width=1.5) fig2.update_layout( paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa', font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155', size=12), xaxis=dict(title='Confidence / Proximity Score', gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11), range=[0.25, 1.02]), yaxis=dict(title='Number of Rows', gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11)), bargap=0.02, margin=dict(t=30, b=50, l=60, r=30), height=360, hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor='#0f172a', font_color='white', font_family=FONT, bordercolor='#1e293b'), ) fig2.show() ``` ```{python} #| echo: false ``` | Score Band | Share of Recognized | |:-----------|--------------------:| | ≥ 0.90 — High confidence | `{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | | 0.70 – 0.90 — Moderate | `{python} f"{100*med_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | | 0.30 – 0.70 — Marginal | `{python} f"{100*low_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % | > **`{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.0f}"`% of all Step 5 recognitions score above 0.90.** The median is 0.99 and the mean is 0.92 — the inference engine is not just broad, it's highly confident on the rows it does resolve. --- *Continue to **Deep Dive** to understand exactly
